Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/SectorAlpha/AlphaGSM/sessions/f0adf689-762e-478f-932b-be0350ded0de

Co-authored-by: cosmosquark <1540682+cosmosquark@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: port picking checks both TCP+UDP, add run_setup_with_port_retry helper

- `pick_free_udp_port()` now loops until the chosen port is bindable on
  both UDP and TCP (without SO_REUSEADDR), mirroring what
  `port_manager.probe_live_listener` checks.  This prevents TIME_WAIT
  TCP sockets left by a previous test in the same batch from causing
  "Live listener already holds 0.0.0.0:<port>" failures in setup.
- `pick_free_tcp_port()` gets the same dual-protocol verification.
- New `_port_free_for_both()` private helper centralises the check.
- New `run_setup_with_port_retry()` public helper provides belt-and-
  suspenders: if setup still fails due to a port conflict it parses
  AlphaGSM's "Recommended free port set" line for the next candidate
  (falling back to a fresh `pick_free_tcp_port()`) and retries up to
  max_tries times.
- Local `_pick_free_port()` in test_tf2.py (UDP-only) and
  test_minecraft_vanilla.py (TCP-only) updated with the same dual check.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/SectorAlpha/AlphaGSM/sessions/52567706-dc79-48ec-a51d-5acbd3abf281

commit.message:
Round 3 deep review: 8 fixes + 23 regression tests

Code fixes:
- server_simulation: initialize proc=None before try to prevent NameError
- builder.py: wrap GeoTIFF tag access in try/except with clear ValueError
- app.py: add explicit return current_values() after Courant failure
- parser_2d: validate elevation array length matches cell count
- server_analysis: show warning notification on empty CSV download
- analysis.py: fix duration overcount (wet_count-1 instead of wet_count)
- builder.py: trace all boundary rings, use longest (multi-ring domains)
- writer_liq: remove unused unique_idx dead code

False positives dropped (4):
- click_to_native formula: verified correct via round-trip tests
- writer_liq unsorted indices: proven sorted by construction
- palette indexing: theoretical only
- temporal arrival inconsistency: by-design behavior

Tests: 23 new in test_round3_fixes.py covering all fixes plus:
- discharge known-answer, degenerate mesh quality, edge functions,
  vorticity known-answer, cross-section values, polygon layer,
  contour saddle point, CRS tighter bounds, flood single-timestep

Total: 334 tests passing (311 existing + 23 new)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Clarify marketplace skills are always team-scoped

Marketplace skills are generic open-source tools that know nothing about
federation. They're always installed into the team's worktree during
onboarding. Meta-squad skills come exclusively from archetype plugin
installs (global scope, auto-discovered by Copilot).

The distinction is structural, not metadata-based — no audience field
needed. Global install = meta-squad learns it. Worktree install = team.

Co-authored-by: Vladi Lyga <lyvladi@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix OpenAPI publish job being skipped on manual workflow dispatch (#45)

https://github.com/SubDynamics/event-hub-api/pull/45

The OpenAPI workflow’s `publish-types-package` job could be skipped on
manual runs due to an overly strict branch gate and string-based input
check. This updates job gating so manual dispatch behaves independently
from push-to-main change detection.

- **Condition split by trigger type**
- Kept push behavior scoped to `main` + detected `packages/types/**`
changes.
- Separated manual dispatch logic so it no longer depends on `github.ref
== refs/heads/main`.

- **Typed manual input evaluation**
- Switched from `github.event.inputs.publish == 'true'` to
`inputs.publish` to align with boolean `workflow_dispatch` inputs.

- **Resulting job predicate**
  ```yaml
  if: >
    (github.event_name == 'push' &&
    github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' &&
    needs.generate-and-validate.outputs.types_changed == 'true') ||
    (github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' && inputs.publish)
  ```

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: SubDynamics <23660334+SubDynamics@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(watch): external capability loading + PID tracker (#918, #921) (#922)

* feat(watch): load external WatchCapabilities from .squad/capabilities/ (#918)

Add loadExternalCapabilities() that scans .squad/capabilities/ for .js
files, validates WatchCapability interface conformance, and registers
them in the CapabilityRegistry after built-in capabilities.

- external-loader.ts: loader with graceful error handling per file
- Wire into runWatch() after createDefaultRegistry()
- Export from watch barrel for downstream consumers
- Sample capability template: issue-pipeline.sample.js
- 6 vitest tests covering all edge cases
- Changeset for minor bump

* fix(watch): track child PIDs and cleanup orphans on exit (#921)

Add PidTracker class that tracks child process PIDs spawned during
watch rounds, kills them on exit/crash, and cleans up stale orphans
from previous crashed runs on startup.

- New pid-tracker.ts with cross-platform process tree kill
- Wire into watch/index.ts for startup cleanup and exit handlers
- Wire into execute.ts to track/untrack copilot session PIDs
- Add pidTracker to WatchContext interface
- Export from barrel for external consumers
- Add .watch-pids to .gitignore (runtime state)
- 11 vitest tests covering track/untrack, cleanup, and stale handling

Closes #921

* fix: address Copilot review comments on PR #922

* security: add startup warning banner + block built-in name hijacking

P0 mitigations from Worf's security review:
1. Print security warning listing all external .js files before importing
2. Block external capabilities from overwriting built-in names
3. New test: hijack attempt is rejected, built-in preserved

Add step segmentation protocol

Implement hierarchical step segmentation as described in Appendix:
- Priority 1: Structural delimiters (\n\n, \n, \\)
- Priority 2: Logic connectors (Therefore, Thus, Hence, So, Consequently)
- Priority 3: Explicit enumerations (Step N:, 1., First,)
- LaTeX math protection (never split inside $...$)
- Max 256 tokens per step to prevent run-on steps

Integrated into:
- MCTS expand: segment multi-step outputs, keep first step only
- format_trajectory: re-segment before numbering for analyst

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: use npm install instead of npm ci for frontend in CI

The lockfile was generated with npm 11.x but CI runs npm 10.x
(Node 22 default). npm ci strictly validates the lockfile and
fails on optional peer dependency resolution differences between
npm versions (esbuild@0.28.0 via vitest/vite 8).

Using npm install is more lenient and handles cross-version
lockfile compatibility.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(jetson): bind-mount host TensorRT/PyCUDA into Docker container

L4T runtime image does not include TensorRT Python bindings or PyCUDA.
Instead of trying to install them inside the container (which needs
nvcc/CUDA dev headers not in runtime image), bind-mount the host's
pre-installed packages into the container at runtime.

setup_jetson.sh now:
- Auto-detects host site-packages location for tensorrt/pycuda
- Adds -v bind-mount flags to the Docker run command in systemd service
- Falls back to mounting full dist-packages if specific dirs not found

Also:
- Add ENV PATH/LD_LIBRARY_PATH in Dockerfile for CUDA visibility
- Make check_ready() create DB dir if missing (os.makedirs)
- Revert pycuda from requirements.txt (host-provided, not pip)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add pension chart utilities and type definitions

- Pension chart utils with type definitions and tests

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: mkForce hyprpaper disable to override nixpalette-hyprland's default

nixpalette-hyprland HM module enables stylix.targets.hyprpaper by default.
Our theming.nix disables it (swww handles wallpaper instead). Use mkForce
to resolve the priority conflict.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Update src/lib/actions/provisioning.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Round 2 deep review: 27 fixes + 24 regression tests

Critical fixes:
- writer_slf: try/finally to prevent file descriptor leak on write errors
- server_simulation: readline timeout (120s), async stop handler with
  terminate-wait-force lifecycle, pysource.local.sh validation
- parser_2d: uint64 overflow check, bounds validation, skip <3 face points
- model.py: negative element index check in Mesh2D.__post_init__

High-priority fixes:
- server_import: TelemacFile try/finally for FD safety
- writer_liq: sort + deduplicate BC times before np.interp
- server_analysis: particle tracing 300s timeout, obs metrics cropped
  to overlapping time window (prevents silent RMSE/NSE corruption)
- telemac_tools/__init__: narrowed bare except to specific exception types

Medium fixes:
- server_playback: speed clamped to [0.1, 10.0]
- server_core: layer extraction returns truncated array on failure,
  CRS offset float() with ValueError guard
- crs.py: UTM zone 1-60 validation, zone-missing warning
- validation.py: empty CSV StopIteration catch, .liq column count check
- domain/builder.py: DEM NaN count warning after interpolation
- server_import: NaN coordinate filter in preview layer

Low-severity cleanup:
- analysis.py: removed dead if-False branch
- constants.py: palette KeyError guard with Viridis fallback
- layers.py: arrow scale and contour saddle point comments
- crs.py: docstring correction

Tests: 24 new regression tests in test_round2_fixes.py
Total: 311 tests passing (287 existing + 24 new)

commit.message:
test: cover global registry list and update branches in CLI

Add three new integration tests in cli_tests.rs to cover uncovered
branches in main.rs:

- install_global_updates_existing_plugin: triggers the else-branch at
  line 583 ("Updated '{spec}' in global registry") by re-installing an
  already-present plugin, which sets added=false in Registry::install.

- list_global_with_installed_plugins: triggers the else-branch at line
  631 ("Globally installed plugins:") by installing two plugins first,
  then running list --global. Also covers:
  - the true-branch (all engines) at line 636 for a no-engine install
  - the false-branch (engine list join) at line 638 for an --engine install

All four were missed by the single existing list_global_no_installed_plugins
test, which only exercised the empty-registry path.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Schema-first workflow for deliverable archetype

Playbook skill:
- Discovery step: now includes data source identification (APIs, repos,
  wikis, telemetry, org charts) → raw/data-sources.json
- Schema Evolution section: schema emerges from first run, evolves with
  domain understanding, becomes a living contract
- Step 3: observe natural data structure during deep-dives
- Step 5: formalize or validate against deliverable.schema.json
- Schema as Skill: once stabilized, create project-level skill documenting
  schema structure, field meanings, quality expectations, edge cases
- Completion criteria: deliverable.schema.json must exist

aggregate.ts:
- --validate flag: validates each deliverable against JSON Schema
- deliverableSchema config field
- Basic structural validation (type, required fields, array items)
- Per-team validation results with item counts
- Invalid deliverables flagged in manifest (validation_warnings)
- branchPrefix default fixed: scan/ → squad/

fix: enable nixpalette at HM level to provide stylix.base16Scheme

With autoImport=false, stylix won't propagate image/base16Scheme from
NixOS to HM automatically. nixpalette's HM module handles this, but only
when nixpalette.enable=true at HM level. Mirror theme/userThemeDir from
osConfig so the HM module configures stylix correctly.

commit.message:
fix(ui): use Activity window flag for keep-screen-on during streaming

Replace View-based keepScreenOn with Activity.window.addFlags(
FLAG_KEEP_SCREEN_ON). The View-based approach only worked while the
StreamScreen composable was visible — navigating to Settings would
clear the flag and allow the screen to lock mid-stream.

The Activity window flag survives in-app navigation since it's set on
the Activity window, not a specific View. The flag is cleared when
streaming stops, the setting is disabled, or the composable disposes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(exp_2411): fix N/A template placeholders in synthesis narrative

Three template failures where prose assumed valid data but got NaN:
- F4 evidence: 'Algorithm gap: N/A mg/dL' → explain unavailable
- Synthesis: 'yields N/A mg/dL — a reassuring convergence' → explain
  transfer model unavailable
- Per-patient: 'span N/A–N/A across 0 patients, suggesting...' →
  acknowledge 18 patients attempted but all crossovers NaN

exp_2421: clean (R² rounding is editorial, Δ=+0.0001 is accurate)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(service): add PARTIAL_WAKE_LOCK to prevent CPU sleep during streaming

Acquire a PARTIAL_WAKE_LOCK when streaming starts and release it when
streaming stops or the service is destroyed. This prevents the CPU from
sleeping when the app is in the background, which would stall the stream.

Uses a 4-hour safety timeout to prevent battery drain from leaked locks.
WAKE_LOCK permission was already declared in the manifest.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
